Works across public cloud, private cloud, and on-premises environments
Optional managed Wazuh Cloud and professional support/training available

Wazuh Cloud and professional services pricing is not publicly listed
Support relies partly on community channels unless a paid support contract is purchased

faqs.answer: Yes, Wazuh is free and open source with no license costs; paid Wazuh Cloud and support services are optional.

mend-io.name: Mend.io
mend-io.short_description: Mend.io is an application security platform offering SCA, SAST, and AI risk governance for enterprise development teams.

features.description: Scans open source dependencies for known vulnerabilities across the codebase.
features.title: Software Composition Analysis (SCA)

features.description: Static application security testing for proprietary code, including AI-generated code security checks.
features.title: High-accuracy SAST

features.description: Surfaces every component in a codebase, including packages, AI models, agents, and system prompts.
features.title: AI-BOM and Shadow AI discovery

features.description: Tests AI models and agents for security risks through automated adversarial testing.
features.title: Automated AI red teaming

features.description: Applies in-app runtime guardrails to reduce prompt injection and related AI risks.
features.title: System prompt hardening

features.description: Automates dependency updates at scale with Merge Confidence ratings and workflows.
features.title: Mend Renovate (automated dependency updates)

features.description: Optional dynamic application security testing and API security testing available as add-ons.
features.title: DAST and API security (add-ons)

pros: Combines traditional application security (SCA/SAST) with AI model and agent security testing in one platform
Automated dependency updates (Mend Renovate) reduce manual patching effort
Used by large enterprises including Microsoft, Google, Vodafone, Yahoo, and Siemens
Publishes transparent per-developer pricing with no additional per-GB fees
cons: Full AppSec suite pricing runs up to $1000 per developer per year, which can be costly for large teams
DAST, API Security, and EOL support are separate add-ons billed at custom pricing rather than included by default
No free plan or free trial is listed; access requires scheduling a demo

faqs.answer: Mend.io is an application security and AI security platform that scans code and dependencies for vulnerabilities and tests AI models and agents for security risks.
faqs.question: What does Mend.io do?

faqs.answer: The Mend.io website does not list a free trial or free plan; it offers a 'Schedule a Demo' option instead.
faqs.question: Does Mend.io offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Mend.io publishes per-developer annual pricing: up to $1000/dev/year for Mend AppSec, up to $300/dev/year for Mend AI, and up to $250/dev/year for Mend Renovate Enterprise.
faqs.question: How is Mend.io priced?

faqs.answer: Mend AI is Mend.io's product for AI-BOM and Shadow AI discovery, system prompt hardening, automated red teaming, and in-app runtime guardrails.
faqs.question: What is Mend AI?

faqs.answer: Mend.io lists enterprise customers including Microsoft, Google, Vodafone, Yahoo, and Siemens.
faqs.question: Who uses Mend.io?

pricing_plans.price: Up to $1000 per developer per year
Up to $300 per developer per year
Up to $250 per developer per year

crowdsec.name: CrowdSec
crowdsec.short_description: CrowdSec is a crowd-sourced, open-source threat intelligence platform that blocks malicious IPs using shared attack data.

features.description: Crowd-sourced IP reputation data shared across a network of more than 70,000 users in 190+ countries.
features.title: Community Blocklist

features.description: Aims to block mass exploitation attempts before they reach protected servers.
features.title: Preemptive Blocking

features.description: Aggregates around 15 million daily signals with roughly 50% daily IP rotation.
features.title: Real-Time Signal Sharing

features.description: Connects with common firewalls and CDNs.
features.title: Plug-and-Play Integrations

features.description: Curated blocklists by industry, tech stack, or country available on the Platinum tier.
features.title: Premium Blocklists

features.description: On-demand IP reputation lookups with contextual threat data and MITRE classifications.
features.title: IP Reputation API

pros: Free, open-source Community Plan with core behavioral detection
Large crowd-sourced network of 70,000+ users across 190+ countries feeding threat data
Claims up to 95% reduction in mass exploitation attempts reaching protected systems
Plug-and-play integration with existing firewalls and CDNs
Multiple pricing tiers let teams pay only for the threat intelligence depth they need
cons: Advanced blocklists and CTI features require paid tiers priced from $1,900+/month for Platinum Blocklists
Full Console Premium pricing is not publicly listed and requires inquiry
Premium support and emergency bug fixes are separately priced add-ons

faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Plan is free and includes behavioral detection and automated response using the open-source Security Engine.
faqs.question: Is CrowdSec free?

faqs.answer: Pricing varies by product; the IP Reputation API starts at $49/month for 5,000 queries, while Platinum Blocklists start around $1,900/month.
faqs.question: How much does CrowdSec's paid tier cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, the CrowdSec Security Engine is open-source software available on GitHub.
faqs.question: Is CrowdSec open source?

faqs.answer: More than 70,000 active users across 190+ countries share roughly 15 million signals daily.
faqs.question: How large is CrowdSec's threat data network?

faqs.answer: Yes, plans can be canceled at any time, effective at the end of the billing month.
faqs.question: Can I cancel a CrowdSec paid plan anytime?

features: Curated blocklists by industry, tech stack, or country
